text: The paper has as its purpose the identification of an interpretative framework concerning the competitiveness of tourism destinations that have a relevant tangible or intangible cultural heritage and that have already experienced development through the setting up of local cultural and creative industries. It will take into account a deepened analysis of the literature concerning tourism destinations with a cultural soul (both at national and international level) and the effects that creative industries can have on the development of regions. In this way it will be possible to have a model that supports the design of a coordinating policy for the management of creative tourism destinations. This research intends to contribute to the huge literature on destination management at an international level, even if, at the national level, it aims at deepening some particular aspects such as the competitiveness of tourism destinations with a cultural heritage and their relationships with cultural and creative industries and the corresponding local development processes. In any case, the aim is to identify an innovative model to which one can refer to with a minimal increase in knowledge, which therefore allows to start a new way of reasoning concerning the competitiveness of tourism destinations. Achieving the aims that have been set for this work will be very important for the scientific development of the research theme and, at the same time, will also have a positive impact on the local development strategies implemented by tourist destinations.
headline: Fully Human Written
